scenarios.request: Using the configure-gtm skill, produce a read-only object-level specification for a Meta Purchase event. The site pushes purchase with ecommerce.items. Didomi exposes didomiVendorsEnabled as its documented comma-delimited string, the verified Meta vendor token is facebook, and strict/basic consent is approved. A compatible Meta base tag already exists. Do not modify a container.

Using the configure-gtm skill, design a read-only GA4 event configuration. The only consent information supplied is that a custom CMP sometimes pushes an enabledVendors value, but its official GTM documentation, exact type, undefined behavior, and vendor identity are unavailable. Do not modify a container.

Using the configure-gtm skill, specify a read-only Meta Purchase implementation. The source event contains ecommerce.items as multiple GA4-shaped objects, while the current official Meta browser contract and installed template require a differently shaped contents array. Strict/basic CMP state is available through a documented direct vendor variable. Do not modify a container.

Using the configure-gtm skill, produce a read-only design for existing GA4 and Google Ads tags. Both products use strict/basic policy, the same documented CMP vendor identity, the same denied logic, compatible Custom Event scope, and the same ownership/change path. Do not modify a container.

Using the configure-gtm skill, specify a read-only TikTok CompletePayment event tag. A verified partner integration already initializes the Pixel and sends the approved page-load event. The request covers only the business event. Do not modify a container.
